items.question_text: Under 8-5-1, what do QNE and QNH stand for in the altitude/flight level transition context?
items.explanation: Per 8-5-1, QNE is the standard altimeter setting and QNH is the local altimeter setting.

choices.text: QNE is standard altimeter setting; QNH is local altimeter setting.

items.question_text: Under 8-5-1, why does the altitude/flight level transition rule apply only below FL 180?
items.explanation: At and above FL180, all aircraft use standard altimeter setting (29.92/QNE), so there is no QNE/QNH difference to manage. Below FL180, some aircraft may still be on local altimeter settings.

choices.text: Because only lateral separation is used below FL180.
Because oceanic control areas do not exist below FL180.
Because at or above FL180 all aircraft use standard altimeter setting, so no QNE/QNH difference exists to compromise separation.
Because QNE and QNH are identical below FL180.

items.question_text: Under 8-5-2, course divergence separation exists when aircraft are established on tracks diverging by at least _____ degrees and at least one aircraft is a specified distance from the point of intersection.
items.explanation: Per 8-5-2, the divergence requirement is 15 degrees for course divergence in transition airspace.

items.question_text: Under 8-5-3, opposite direction aircraft using the transition procedure must be vertically separated until they have passed each other by at least _____ miles, unless another form of separation is established earlier.
items.explanation: Per 8-5-3, opposite direction aircraft must pass each other by a specified distance before vertical separation may be discontinued.

items.question_text: Under 8-5-4, same direction aircraft in the transition area must have the applicable longitudinal minima applied based on the _____ of the two aircraft.
items.explanation: Per 8-5-4, same direction longitudinal minima in transition airspace are based on the speed of the two aircraft.

items.question_text: Under 8-5-5, radar identification procedures from Chapter _____ may be applied in offshore/oceanic transition airspace where radar coverage exists.
items.explanation: Per 8-5-5, Chapter 5 radar identification procedures apply where coverage is adequate.

items.question_text: When two aircraft cross the offshore boundary below FL180 and one is on QNH while the other is on QNE, what separation risk can result if the altimeter-setting difference is not managed?

choices.text: Lateral separation is automatically substituted for vertical separation.
Nothing — QNE and QNH always produce identical altitude readings.
Vertical separation could be compromised because the same indicated altitude may represent different true altitudes.
Both aircraft will automatically adjust to the same setting upon crossing the boundary.

items.explanation: Per 8-5-1, differences between QNE and QNH altimeter settings can compromise vertical separation, so control action must ensure separation is maintained during the transition.
